disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A well-defined chalk-white patch at the corner of the mouth, noticed tonight while removing makeup
trigger_question_examples: What did you notice about your skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The skin at the corner of my mouth has gone completely white, like all the colour has drained out of it. I saw it tonight when I took my makeup off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion is completely painless and not itchy
trigger_question_examples: Does the patch hurt or itch at all?
Is there any pain when you touch it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't hurt at all and it isn't itchy — that's almost the strangest part, it just looks awful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A matching small white spot on a fingertip, noticed on inspection — suggesting a distal/facial distribution rather than a single new lesion
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any pale patches anywhere else?
Any changes on your hands or elsewhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Actually, now that I'm looking, there's a tiny white spot on one of my fingertips too. I hadn't paid attention to it before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Redness immediately around the patch appeared only after the patient scrubbed the area vigorously in panic
trigger_question_examples: The redness around it — when did that appear?
Have you rubbed or scrubbed the area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I did scrub at it really hard when I panicked because I wanted it to come off. The redness came up after that — it wasn't red before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No exposure to strong chemicals, bleaches or industrial agents on the skin
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any chemicals, bleach or strong cleaning products on that skin?
Any new products or exposures on your face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that. Just my normal makeup and cleanser — no bleach or chemicals or anything industrial.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patch has no scale, crust, blistering, ulceration or bleeding; the skin surface looks intact
trigger_question_examples: Is there any flaking, scaling or crust on the patch?
Is the skin broken, weeping or bleeding anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No flaking or crust that I can see, and the skin isn't broken — it looks smooth, just white.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, and the patient feels systemically well
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel unwell in yourself, any fever or feeling hot and shivery?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, I don't feel sick or unwell at all in myself — I'm just frightened about how my face looks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked acute distress and catastrophic fear of a serious infection or disfigurement, driving frantic scrubbing and an urge to attend an emergency department tonight
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling about this?
What are you most worried it could be?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm terrified. I keep thinking it's a flesh-eating infection or that my face is going to be ruined. I was about to drive to the emergency department.
